diffCoverage.notes: Root cause confirmed directly in the pinned zca-rust rev c95c88b (src/listen.rs: `if message.is_self && !self_listen { continue; }` drops self messages before delivery). The bug-relevant change is the single login_and_listen call-site flip to zalo::login_with(.., true); the rest of the command/mod.rs diff is separate in-flight feature work outside this task's scope.

findings.evidence: Evidence bundle status=pass with attested build/tests/lint/structural checks (cargo test 35 passed, 0 failed; clippy -D warnings clean) and a non-secret live smoke artifact (self-listen-render-verified) recording that a message sent from the original Zalo app on another device renders in zca-desktop as an outgoing bubble, while a send from this app still renders exactly once. Architecture + reliability reviewers pass. The same-device echo double-render risk is covered by the pre-existing store + frontend dedupe; self messages do not bump unread (bump_unread=!is_self).

summary: fix-self-listen-outgoing is honestly done. Root cause (production login path used self_listen=false, so zca-rust dropped every is_self event) was confirmed against the pinned upstream source and fixed with a single forward-only call-site change in the command layer (zalo::login_with(.., true)). No new layer, no new public API, no secret/IPC change. Build/test/lint are green and the owner-verified live smoke proves self-authored messages from another device now render once without inflating unread. Pass.
